title: Comparing Your Real Options for PayPal Alternatives for Nigerian Creators
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal and Stripe block creators in dozens of countries. Here is the infrastructure that works everywhere they don't.

content.text: Nigerian digital creators need a PayPal alternative due to platform restrictions. If you can't sell digital products using PayPal in Nigeria, you're not alone. Many countries face similar obstacles with popular payment processors. In this post, we'll explore the real options for Nigerian creators to sell digital products without PayPal. We'll compare the alternatives, highlighting their strengths and weaknesses. If you're struggling to find a payment solution, this post will help you make an informed decision. By the end, you'll know which option to choose for your digital business.
What Each Option Actually Offers

PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip are popular options for digital creators, but they have significant limitations in Nigeria. PayPal restricts access to many African countries, including Nigeria, due to high transaction risks. Stripe, while present in Nigeria, requires a physical address for verification, which can be difficult for many creators. Gumroad blocks certain countries, including Nigeria, due to high chargeback rates. Payhip also restricts access to some countries. In contrast, the Core Shop Engine offers a full backend with automated delivery, payment verification, and PDF receipts. It requires no payment processor account and works everywhere.
How They Handle Restricted Countries

When it comes to handling restricted countries, the alternatives fall short. PayPal's restrictions are well-known, while Stripe's verification process is a significant hurdle. Gumroad's block list is long, and Payhip's restrictions can be unpredictable. In contrast, the Core Shop Engine is designed to work with NOWPayments, a payment processor that accepts 300+ coins and requires no KYC or buyer account. This means Nigerian creators can sell digital products without worrying about platform restrictions.
Payment Speed, Fees, and Real Costs

When it comes to payment speed, fees, and real costs, the alternatives vary significantly. PayPal's fees can be high, especially for international transactions. Stripe's fees are competitive, but the verification process can be slow. Gumroad's fees are transparent, but the block list can impact sales. Payhip's fees are low, but the restrictions can be unpredictable. In contrast, the Core Shop Engine offers a flat fee of $49.99, with no hidden costs or subscription fees. Payment speed is fast, and the automated delivery system ensures a seamless customer experience.
PayPal's restrictions in Nigeria are a major obstacle for digital creators.
Automation and Delivery Capabilities

The risk of platform dependency is asymmetric in a specific way that most creators underestimate. When PayPal or Stripe is working, everything seems fine. When it stops working — through suspension, policy change, or geographic restriction enforcement — you lose access to your revenue channel instantly, often with funds already inside the platform that you cannot withdraw. Rebuilding on a new platform takes weeks. The income gap during that period is real money lost. Crypto-native infrastructure removes this asymmetric risk. Your wallet cannot be suspended. Your GitHub Pages store cannot be taken down by a payment platform's compliance team. The infrastructure is under your control from day one, which means no single external decision can cut off your income.

The flat fee of $49.99 is a bargain compared to the alternatives, and the lack of subscription fees means you can sell digital products without worrying about ongoing costs.
